name: Thin Scaloppine
intro: I like to serve this dish with halved cherry tomatoes and some rocket scattered over the top for both taste and colour.
method: Get your butcher to cut the scaloppine quite thick and then, with a meat tenderiser, flatten them into a round shape, as big as a plate. Dust the surface of the meat with a little flour, coat with some beaten egg, then press the meat into the breadcrumbs.

Melt the butter in a frying pan and add the oil and sage leaves. When the butter foams, cook the scaloppine well on both sides until golden. Season with salt and pepper, and serve with a few wedges of lemon, or add some rocket and cherry tomatoes.
